rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-based assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to ensure scope compatibility.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referencing component ports must reside in valid component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference only visible and properly typed AADL features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Constants and equations in AGREE should use visible AADL identifiers and types.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented via valid variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
AGREE syntax notes for annex placement
Defensive rules for type mismatches and property references
